features.title: Bootstrap external references (AI SDK skill + llms.txt)
features.planner_notes: Fetch the official AI SDK Claude Code skill from Vercel's repo and place at .claude/skills/ai-sdk/SKILL.md. Fetch ai-sdk.dev/llms.txt to docs/references/ai-sdk-llms.txt. If exact URLs change, search ai-sdk.dev/docs/getting-started or vercel/ai GitHub repo for current locations. Fallback: use the inline patterns in CLAUDE.md § AI SDK v6 idioms section as the reference.
acceptance_criteria: .claude/skills/ai-sdk/SKILL.md exists with non-empty content
docs/references/ai-sdk-llms.txt exists with non-empty content (or a stub explaining manual fetch)
If fetch fails, status set to accepted_with_warning and warning logged

features.title: Validate taxonomy.json against schema and DAG rules
features.planner_notes: Read taxonomy.json. Write scripts/validate_taxonomy.ts that checks: (a) 25 concepts present, (b) each has required fields, (c) categories are foundational/interpretive/communication, (d) prerequisites form a DAG with no cycles, (e) every prerequisite references an existing concept id.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/validate_taxonomy.ts exists and runs via bun run
Script exits 0 when taxonomy is valid
Script exits 1 with specific error messages when invalid
All 25 concepts in taxonomy.json pass validation

features.title: Verify Zod schemas parse all four exemplars
features.planner_notes: Schemas exist at lib/schemas/content-schemas.ts. Write a test that loads each of the four exemplars and parses it through the matching Zod schema. All four must parse without errors.
acceptance_criteria: Test file exists at lib/schemas/__tests__/exemplar-validation.test.ts
ConceptPrimerFrontmatter parses central-tendency.md frontmatter
DictionaryEntryFrontmatter parses median.md frontmatter
ScenarioSchema parses central-tendency-tier-2.json
SyntheticDatasetSchema parses hgse_cohort_salaries.json
All four assertions pass

features.title: Generate 24 remaining concept primers (Gemini Content Generator)
features.planner_notes: Use Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite via @ai-sdk/google with thinkingLevel='medium'. For each concept in taxonomy.json except 'central-tendency', generate a primer following the exact structure of content/concepts/central-tendency.md. Use Gal 2002 framework grounding. Avoid calculation-heavy examples. Reading examples must be plausibly Harvard-staff-relevant. Visible thinking routine must match the routine declared in the concept's primer frontmatter.
builder_rules: One file per concept at content/concepts/<id>.md
Match exemplar structure: hook, three-part breakdown, Harvard-relevant 'why this matters' section, reading example, visible-thinking closer
Frontmatter must validate against ConceptPrimerFrontmatter schema
No formulas anywhere (regex /=|\+|\*|sum\(|avg\(/ returns no matches)
Word count between 400 and 700 (roughly matches exemplar)
Use the model defaults from CLAUDE.md § LLM call configuration per role
acceptance_criteria: 24 new files exist in content/concepts/
Each parses against ConceptPrimerFrontmatter via validate_primers script
LLM-judge score ≥ 3.5/5 against central-tendency.md anchor on (clarity, level, visible-thinking)
No formula syntax in any primer
Each references at least one prerequisite from its taxonomy entry
features.test_command: bun run scripts/validate_primers.ts && bun run scripts/judge_primers.ts

features.title: Generate 50 dictionary entries (Gemini Content Generator)
features.planner_notes: Generate 50 dictionary entries: one per taxonomy concept (25), plus 25 adjacent terms that appear in scenarios but aren't full concepts. Follow median.md structure. school_usage notes are inferred ('common in...', 'typically used for...'), not asserted. Cover HGSE, HBS, FAS, HMS, SEAS minimum per entry.
acceptance_criteria: 50 files in content/dictionary/
Each parses against DictionaryEntryFrontmatter schema
Every related_terms[] reference points to an existing dictionary entry or concept primer id
School_usage block covers at least 4 of: HGSE, HBS, FAS, HMS, SEAS
Plain-language definition under 25 words

features.title: Implement 5 parametric synthetic dataset generators
features.planner_notes: Implement 5 generators in scripts/generators/: cohort_outcomes (already has output exemplar — use as reference), two_group_comparison, time_series_with_break, survey_categorical, paired_pre_post. Each must emit JSON conforming to SyntheticDatasetSchema. pedagogical_metadata.features must reflect actual statistical features computed from the rows. Generators are pure deterministic code — NO LLM calls.
builder_rules: Generators are deterministic given a seed parameter
Every generator declares its tunable pedagogical features (skew, outliers, confounding, etc.)
All values synthetic; plausibility_notes[] declares the synthetic boundary

Output passes validate_exemplar_consistency.ts — note this validator is currently hardcoded to the salary_usd field from the cohort_outcomes exemplar; F005 must generalize it to accept a configurable numeric field per dataset (read pedagogical_metadata to discover the field), so the four new generators (two_group_comparison, time_series_with_break, survey_categorical, paired_pre_post) can be checked against their own numeric columns
No LLM calls in generators — pure TypeScript
acceptance_criteria: 5 scripts in scripts/generators/
Each runs via 'bun run scripts/generators/<n>.ts > /tmp/test.json' producing valid JSON
Output matches SyntheticDatasetSchema
pedagogical_metadata.features values verifiable from rows (validator passes)

features.title: Generate scenarios for foundational concepts (5 tiers × 8 = 40 scenarios)
features.planner_notes: For each of the 8 foundational concepts, generate 5 scenarios (one per tier). Tier 1 = MCQ comprehension check, tier 2 = MCQ with always-visible hint, tier 3 = short answer with prompt scaffolds, tier 4 = long answer with no scaffolding, tier 5 = wiki draft prompt. Reference datasets generated in F005 where appropriate. Maintain mathematical consistency: stats in artifact match dataset rows. Use Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite with thinkingLevel='medium' and JSON response mode.
builder_rules: Tier-appropriate question_type: tier 1 → multiple_choice, tier 2 → multiple_choice_with_hint, tier 3 → short_answer, tier 4 → long_answer, tier 5 → wiki_draft
Visible thinking scaffold matches the routine declared in the concept's primer frontmatter
If dataset_ref is set, scenario artifact stats must match dataset stats within $100
acceptance_criteria: 40 files in content/scenarios/
Each parses against ScenarioSchema
All dataset_ref values point to existing datasets
validate_exemplar_consistency.ts passes
LLM-judge score ≥ 3.5/5 against central-tendency-tier-2.json anchor
features.test_command: bun run scripts/validate_scenarios.ts && bun run scripts/judge_scenarios.ts

features.title: Bootstrap Vue 3 frontend skeleton with AI SDK Vue v2
features.planner_notes: Do NOT block on F000. The inline AI SDK v6 idioms in CLAUDE.md § 'AI SDK v6 idioms' are sufficient to scaffold the Vue app. If .claude/skills/ai-sdk/SKILL.md and docs/references/ai-sdk-llms.txt exist, prefer them as a deeper reference; otherwise proceed with the inline section. Create frontend/ via 'npm create vite@latest frontend -- --template vue-ts'. Install: @ai-sdk/vue@^2.0.0, ai@^6.0.0, @ai-sdk/google@^2.0.0, shadcn-vue, tailwindcss, vue-router, fuse.js, marked, gray-matter. Set up routing for: /module/:concept_id, /dictionary, /demo/autoplay. Build LMSShell component (breadcrumb, lesson counter, progress bar).
acceptance_criteria: frontend/ contains valid Vue 3 + Vite + TS app
package.json pins @ai-sdk/vue to ^2.0.0 and ai to ^6.0.0
npm run dev starts without errors
Three routes accessible and render placeholders
LMSShell component renders breadcrumb and progress
Tailwind classes work
npm run build succeeds
features.test_command: cd frontend && npm run build && npm run lint

features.title: Implement Scaffold Selector function
features.planner_notes: Pure function in lib/scaffold-selector.ts. Takes (mastery: number, history: Interaction[]) and returns one of the 5 component names. Implement thresholds from CLAUDE.md exactly. Edge cases: mastery exactly at 0.20 → tier 2 (use ≥, not >). Tests cover all 5 transitions plus edges.
acceptance_criteria: lib/scaffold-selector.ts exports pickScaffold(state) function
Tests cover all 5 tier transitions (5 cases)
Tests cover all 4 boundaries (4 cases)
All tests pass
Function has no side effects (pure)

features.title: Build Dictionary viewer (standalone page + inline popover)
features.planner_notes: Two surfaces: (a) standalone DictionaryView.vue page at /dictionary with searchable list (Fuse.js), term detail with school_usage table; (b) DictionaryPopover.vue component for inline term mentions in scenario prose. Term detection: any word matching a dictionary entry's `term` field becomes a hover-target. Popover shows plain_definition; click opens full term page.
acceptance_criteria: DictionaryView renders all 50 terms with search
Search uses Fuse.js, filters on term + plain_definition + related_terms
Term detail shows full content with school_usage table
DictionaryPopover renders on hover for any matched term in scenario prose
Click on popover navigates to /dictionary/<term>
All keyboard navigation accessible (focus, escape closes popover)

features.title: Wire backend API with Hono and Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite via @ai-sdk/google
features.planner_notes: Hono server in backend/. Three endpoints: POST /api/chat (streaming via streamText with @ai-sdk/google + gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview), POST /api/state/infer, POST /api/quality/eval. Tool definitions for streamText must match BOTH the 5 scaffold component names AND the 2 visualization tool names exactly: WorkedExample, ScaffoldedMCQ, GuidedShortAnswer, BareLongAnswer, WikiDraft, render_chart, render_flowchart. Tool input schemas come from DESIGN.md § RenderedChart and § RenderedFlowchart — the Vue components receive tool input as props with no JSON re-parsing. CORS configurable via env. Provider initialization reads GEMINI_API_KEY from env; HARVARD_PORTAL_GEMINI_BASE_URL is a v1.5 swap and not used in v1. Per-role thinkingLevel from CLAUDE.md § LLM call configuration. Set max_review_rounds=4 for this feature — tool name drift is a silent failure mode worth extra rounds to catch.
acceptance_criteria: Backend starts via 'bun run backend/index.ts'
POST /api/chat streams a response with tool calls
Tool definitions match scaffold names exactly: WorkedExample, ScaffoldedMCQ, GuidedShortAnswer, BareLongAnswer, WikiDraft
Tool definitions also include render_chart and render_flowchart with input schemas from DESIGN.md § RenderedChart and § RenderedFlowchart
render_chart and render_flowchart tool calls produce inline visualizations rendered as message.parts; both require non-empty caption and provenance fields
POST /api/state/infer returns valid LearnerState JSON
POST /api/quality/eval returns rubric scores
All API keys read from env; no hardcoded secrets
Errors return JSON with status codes (not HTML)
CORS allows configurable origin (LMS iframe)
Provider config supports both direct Google API and Harvard portal baseURL
